title: Acyclicity
lean_code_with_comments:
def IsAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ v ‚ąą G, ¬¨ ‚ąÉ p : Walk G v v, p.IsDirectedWalk ‚ąß p.length ‚Č• 1
lean_code_without_comments:
def IsAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ v ‚ąą G, ¬¨ ‚ąÉ p : Walk G v v, p.IsDirectedWalk ‚ąß p.length ‚Č• 1
lean_explanation:
The predicate `IsAcyclic` takes one explicit argument `G : CDMG Node` ‚ÄĒ the CDMG whose acyclicity is being asserted ‚ÄĒ and lives over the ambient implicit binders `Node : Type*` with `[DecidableEq Node]`. The type parameter `Node` is the carrier the LN treats as the silent universe of vertex labels; the decidability instance is load-bearing because the downstream `Walk.IsDirectedWalk` predicate (from `def_3_4`) recurses on per-edge `a ‚ąą G.E` membership checks, and the `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance from `def_3_2` reduces `v ‚ąą G` to `Finset.mem` on `G.J ‚ą™ G.V` ‚ÄĒ both require `DecidableEq Node` to type-check.

The body reads `‚ąÄ v ‚ąą G, ¬¨ ‚ąÉ p : Walk G v v, p.IsDirectedWalk ‚ąß p.length ‚Č• 1`. Three Mathlib-style idioms are worth flagging. First, `‚ąÄ v ‚ąą G` is sugar for `‚ąÄ v, v ‚ąą G ‚Üí ‚Ä¶`; the `v ‚ąą G` notation is *not* Lean primitive but is supplied by the `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance defined in `CDMGNotation.lean`, where it unfolds to `v ‚ąą G.J ‚ą™ G.V`. The LN's literal phrase "for any node $v \in J \cup V$" therefore transports verbatim onto the Lean text. Second, `Walk G v v` is the dependent type of walks indexed on both endpoints: "a walk from $v$ to itself" is encoded directly in the type, with no side condition threading a "starts at $v$, ends at $v$" hypothesis through. Third, `p.IsDirectedWalk` is a `Prop`-valued predicate witnessing that every edge of `p` is a directed edge (from `G.E`), and `p.length` is the edge count via `Walk.nil ‚Ü¶ 0` / `Walk.cons ‚Ü¶ length p + 1`.

The overall shape: a single existential is *negated* under the `‚ąÄ v ‚ąą G` quantifier, and the existential bundles the walk together with two independent constraints ‚ÄĒ directedness and non-triviality. "Non-trivial" is pinned to $n \ge 1$ per the operator clarification accompanying the LN block, so a length-$1$ directed self-loop $v \rightharpoonup v$ counts as a forbidden cycle.
design_choices:
**Quantifier scope `‚ąÄ v ‚ąą G`, mirroring the LN's literal `v \in J \cup V`.** The `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance unfolds `v ‚ąą G` to `v ‚ąą G.J ‚ą™ G.V`, so the Lean text matches the LN word-for-word with no helper notation. A narrower scope `‚ąÄ v ‚ąą G.V` would be logically equivalent ‚ÄĒ the `J`-half is vacuous because `def_3_1`'s `hE_subset` forces every directed edge's target into `G.V`, and `hJV_disj` rules out a return to any `j ‚ąą G.J` ‚ÄĒ but it would drift away from the LN's literal phrasing and force downstream rows to carry an extra $J$-versus-$V$ case split whenever they destructure a hypothesis about "every node of $G$".

**"Non-trivial" encoded as `p.length ‚Č• 1`.** The LN block uses "non-trivial" without a definition; the operator's addition pins it to walks of length $n \ge 1$ ‚ÄĒ at least one edge traversed ‚ÄĒ and admits a self-loop $v \rightharpoonup v$ as a non-trivial walk of length $1$. The wording surfaced two plausible readings (length $\ge 1$ versus length $\ge 2$, only the latter would let an acyclic CDMG carry self-loops); we resolve to the first, and the rewritten tex spec spells out the no-self-loop consequence in its "Consequence" paragraph. Alternative encodings `0 < p.length`, `¬¨ p.length = 0`, or `p ‚Č† Walk.nil v hv` are logically equivalent but `p.length ‚Č• 1` reads most directly off the LN's `$n \ge 1$`.

**`Walk G v v` rather than a fresh `Cycle` or `Path` type.** The upstream `Walk` inductive (`def_3_4`) indexes on endpoints, so "from $v$ to itself" lives in the *type* ‚ÄĒ no side condition required. A separate `Cycle G v` type would force a forgetful coercion at every downstream site that mixes acyclicity with general-walk reasoning (topological order in `def_3_8`, the acyclic-iff-topological-order claim in `claim_3_2`, and the $d$-/$\sigma$-separation arguments in later chapters). A `Path` type with no repeated vertices would *exclude* the LN-admitted length-$1$ self-loop case ‚ÄĒ the wrong shape for this row.

**Conjunction under the existential, not a subtype or bundled `DirectedCycle` struct.** The LN prose says "a non-trivial directed walk" ‚ÄĒ two independent constraints on the same walk. A plain `‚ąß` keeps inversion ergonomic: a hypothesis `¬¨ ‚ąÉ p, p.IsDirectedWalk ‚ąß p.length ‚Č• 1` decomposes by `rintro ‚ü®p, hp_dir, hp_len‚ü©` with no `Subtype.mk` / `Subtype.property` unpacking, exactly the shape that downstream consumers want when constructing or rejecting witness cycles.

**Mathlib re-use.** Built on this project's own `Walk` from `def_3_4`. Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.Walk` is for undirected single-channel graphs and has no notion of directed-versus-bidirected edges or a $J$/$V$ input/output partition, so it cannot encode a CDMG walk. Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.IsAcyclic` forbids only undirected cycles of length $\ge 3$, missing both the directed-walk constraint and the length-$1$ self-loop case. Rolling our own predicate on top of our own `Walk` is the only option that stays close to the LN.
